summary: Connection timeout isn't supported
description: The connection timeout isn't supported yet

summary: data loss while writing concurrently
description:
this is my testcase - https://gist.github.com/3987628

while one process, it works all right
while two process, it will lose half the data

summary: please update description of 'fsync' write concern flag
description:
Since your driver's documentation (e.g. [here|http://api.mongodb.org/c/current/write_concern.html]) describes the 'fsync' write concern flag, can you please update its description so that it accurately describes the current behavior, which is:

# 'fsync' is identical to 'j' if mongod is running with a journal (today, 99% of users should be here)
# 'fsync' requests that the write be sync()'d to disk only if mongod is not running with a journal
# 'fsync' cannot be used simultaneously with the 'j' flag

The point is that:
- 'j' means 'sync via the journal if present, or error if not, or if 'fsync' is also set'
- 'fsync' means 'sync via the journal if present, or sync via msync() if there is no journal, and error if 'j' is also set'

Additionally, could you please mark the 'fsync' flag as "Deprecated; do not use", following this commit [here|https://github.com/mongodb/docs/commit/75c648730b54a920cf85372fd27ab8600b67b794]

Please see SERVER-11399 for background.

Thanks!

summary: Support the Exhaust flag on query
bson_init_static should take size_t for length
description: bson_init_from_static takes a bson_uint32_t which is inconsistent with other functions. Use size_t.

summary: bson_reinit() should reuse existing malloc'd buffer
description: To help under situations where malloc is likely, bson_reinit should simply keep the existing buffer around so we don't go through the inline/malloc transition a second time.

summary: try to maintain existing connections when reconnecting to replicaset members
description:
Currently, when trying to make a cluster in replicaset mode healthy, we rerun the entire connection process.

This means we:

 1) break existing connections, that may not be necessary
 2) discard ping time information

Both are not ideal and should be fixed.

summary: For C++, <mongoc-read-prefs.h> has an error.
in example-client.c arguments of mongoc_client_get_collection are not given in proper order
description:
-   collection = mongoc_client_get_collection (client, collection_name, "test");
+   collection = mongoc_client_get_collection (client, "test", collection_name);

summary: example-client.c only returns one answer
description:
I am using a collection with many documents 
and when I run example-client.c it only displays the 
first document. If I use the skip parameter in example-client.c 
I can see that other documents are present in the db collection.

summary: mongoc_collection_save doesn't insert documents with user set id's
description:
Follow the semantics on http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/method/db.collection.save/

Save should upsert the document if it already has an id (currently uses MONGOC_UPDATE_NONE instead of MONGOC_UPDATE_UPSERT)

Also save should replace the document entirely and not $set fields

I attached an untested version of mongoc_collection_save that I think should work.

summary: IPV6 compile-time flag
Add find_and_modify example
description: We need an example of how to run a findAndModify command since it is so common.l

summary: support rdtscp for counters
description:
The counter code needs to be cleaned up a bit. We need to merge the prototype that allows for --enable-rdtscp. On Windows, we should investigate the use of shared memory or other systems for counter access out of process.

The counters should fallback to atomic int if sched_getcpu() and rdtscp are not available.

summary: add proper TestSuite
description:
We need to create a TestSuite abstraction that allows us to do a bunch of fancy stuff with the unit tests.

For example, tests should by default run in a fork()'d child (except on Windows). They should track timing and success (pass/fail/skip). If possible, we should try to ptrace() and get a stack trace upon failure.

summary: Add function to get database/collection names from their structures
description: Sort of an oversight, add function to extract the ->name fields from the opaque structures.

summary: add bson_init_from_json()
track unit tests in `git notes`
description:
it would be really nice if build systems kept the result of the build and unit tests in a `git notes` using their build name for the notes group (build-centos06 for example).

If these then get pushed to github, we can track them from anywhere and graph performance, build status, etc over time.

summary: bson_json_reader_t does not work with trailing \n on files.
need support for MSVC / Windows
description:
First, let me say that I think this re-write of the C driver is a great thing.  One feature that I have not seen addressed, however, is Windows (MSVC) compatibility.  

I am happy to help in this regard if such help would not be unwelcome.

summary: openssl multi thread support
description:
openssl is only thread safe if

* CRYPTO_set_locking_callback()
* CRYPTO_set_id_callback()

have been invoked.

These should be added portably to _mongoc_ssl_init

summary: no accessor functions for collections in gridfs
description:
A c-driver client can't currently access the collections "files" and "chunks" in the mongoc_gridfs_t. Accessing these is important in case a client wants to refine the semantics of files deletions (example e.g. in the nsfmongo driver ("git clone git://alice.wu-wien.ac.at/nsf" see nsf/library/mongodb/nsfmongo.c, code around MONGO_HAS_NO_GRIDFS_COLLECTION_ACCESSOR). Providing an accessor just for reading access should be quite harmless. Without the patch, a client has to include the private header files.

Attached is a patch relative to the current head version of the mongo-c-driver.

summary: Allow access to getLastError document
description:
There is no way to access the getLastError document, since it's being received, evaluated and then discarded right after _mongoc_write_concern_has_gle().

So, clients can't access the rest of the fields to evaluate for example, how many documents were affected by the previous update/delete operation, or whether an upsert resulted in an update or an insert, besides other useful fields I've seen there.

summary: Log a warning when ignoring user-specified read preference
0.92.0 won't pass make test?
description:
Upgrading from 0.90.0 to 0.92.0 gave me problems, so I uninstalled libbson 0.400 and mongoc 0.90.0, and made a fresh install.
Make test didn't work, there seems to be a missing file. Was it just me?

{quote}
\# git clone https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver.git mongo-c-diver-0.92.0
\[... all fine...]
\# cd mongo-c-diver-0.92.0
\# git checkout 0.92.0
HEAD is now at 0163ed7... build: bump for 0.92.0!
\# ./autogen.sh
\[... all fine...]
\# make && make install
\[... all fine...]
\# make test
\[... tests/make_ca.pl ./tests/trust_dir ./tests/trust_dir.cnf
Generating RSA private key, 1024 bit long modulus ... ... ... ... ]
\[ ... ... ]
Write out database with 1 new entries
Data Base Updated
Can't exec "c_rehash": No such file or directory at tests/make_ca.pl line 89.
failed: -1 at tests/make_ca.pl line 89.
make: *** \[tests/trust_dir/done] Error 2
\#
{quote}

summary: _mongoc_cursor_new should not assert on configuration options
description:
There are some assertions in _mongoc_cursor_new that could be hit if the URI was dynamically set (or from a configuration file) in the users application.

It should instead set a permanent error and return a valid cursor and then fail on the first call to _next().

snip ...

{code}
101    /*
102     * TODO: These two following assertions should be runtime catchable since
103     *       they rely on system configuration that could be dynamic.
104     *       Change to set internal error and return a valid cursor that
105     *       fails on first _next() call.
106     */
107 
108    /* we can't have exhaust queries with limits */
109    BSON_ASSERT (!((flags & MONGOC_QUERY_EXHAUST) && limit));
110 
111    /* we can't have exhaust queries with sharded clusters */
112    BSON_ASSERT (!((flags & MONGOC_QUERY_EXHAUST) && client->cluster.isdbgrid));
{code}

summary: Tutorial examples do not compile
description: See https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/libmongo-client/+bug/1078858 for details and patch. Note to change makefile and documentation.

summary: Create extended test suite (long-running tests, replica set reconnect tests, etc.)
add is_alive for mongoc_cursor_t
description: mongoc_cursor_is_alive() should be added to match parity with other drivers.

summary: add helper to get max bson and message size.
description: other drivers have feature to get the server provided max bson and message size. we should expose this via the mongoc_client_t.

summary: add collection validate helper
description: other drivers have Collection.validate() helpers. We should add one as well.

summary: check types of $explain and $snapshot in cursors
description: no type checking occurs currently, go ahead and check these from the bson query document.

summary: propagate w= from uri string
description: We currently do not propagate the w= parameter from the uri string. this should be fixed.

summary: mongoc_write_concern_t should support tag name for w=
description:
currently only w=majority and w=-1..N are supported.

we need to pass valid utf8 as the name of the tag for w=.

summary: propagate safe= from the uri string
description: we do not currently propagate the safe= parameter from the uri string. this should be fixed.

summary: Missing check for data type of _id field in gridfs files collection
description: If _id field type in <gridfs>.files collection is not ObjectID, driver crashes with Segmentation fault in file mongoc-gridfs-file.c on function _mongoc_gridfs_file_new_from_bson.

summary: allow getting hint for node from client before submitting command
manage min and max wire version on per connection basis
description:
currently min/max wire version is negotiated as a cluster in libmongoc. This is actually wrong and should be on a per-node basis.

using preselect will allow collection/cursors to determine how to communicate with the peer using the maximum feature set.

summary: mongoc_collection_aggregate should support non-zero batchSize
description:
This involves improving mongoc-cursor-cursorid to special case responses that include firstBatch as non-zero sized arrays.

This will result in an ABI break to pass arbitrary options.

summary: mongoc_collection_aggregate should support allowDiskUse option
description: Currently, there is no way to pass options to aggregate. This should be fixed, but requires an ABI break (which is okay for now).

summary: mongoc_client_t should have a serverStatus command helper
description: Other drivers have a helper to execute the serverStatus command. This should be added to mongoc_client_t.

summary: add function to access current document in mongoc_cursor_t
description: A function used to exist to access the current document from the cursor after mongoc_cursor_next() has been called (and in other drivers). The new C driver should have it too.

summary: lower gridfs chunk size to 255k
description: Drivers are lowering the chunk size to 255k so they will fit within power of two sizes on the server.

summary: Support canonicalizeHostname from URI option.
description: Both C# and Java drivers support canonicalizeHostname=true to resolve hostname from the peer address before initializing GSSAPI. This is useful for situations where aliases are used for shorted hostnames and the underlying GSSAPI not performing this for us.

summary: typo in error message
add find_and_modify helper to mongoc_collection_t
description: Currently, there is an example for doing find and modify, and it's sort of unnecessary to copy into users code base. Let's add a proper helper into mongoc_collection_t that we will maintain/test/etc.

summary: mongoc_collection_t should use createIndexes command when available
description: There is a new command for creating indexes, and it should be used when the wire version is new enough to use it.

summary: fallback to legacy ops when using unacknowledged writes
description: If the user is requesting unacknowledged writes for an insert/update/delete, we should fallback to using legacy OP_INSERT, OP_UPDATE, and OP_DELETE if the server supports them (minWireVersion of 0).

summary: Add support for Sun C Compiler (Solaris 10+)
description: It would be nice if Sun's C Compiler (check with __SUNPRO_C) was supported for libbson and mongo-c-driver. This will allow us to compile in more situations where gcc is not, or cannot be installed.

summary: add configure check __sync_add_and_fetch_8()
description: Not all platforms support all of the compiler intrinsic features of GCC. For example, older PPC-based mac mini's do not include 64-bit atomics.
